title: Authenticated RCE via POST /api/v1/node-custom-function (arbitrary JS execution, NodeVM sandbox escape)
description: In Flowise <= 3.1.1, POST /api/v1/node-custom-function is registered in packages/server/src/routes/node-custom-functions/index.ts as router.post('/', nodesRouter.executeCustomFunction) with no permission middleware. Any authenticated user or API-key holder can submit arbitrary JavaScript (body.javascriptFunction) to the Custom JS Function node. When E2B_APIKEY is not configured (the default), executeJavaScriptCode in packages/components/src/utils.ts silently falls back to the @flowiseai/nodevm sandbox, which can be escaped via an Error object whose constructor chain resolves to the host realm, recovering the host Function constructor and process.getBuiltinModule('child_process') to run system commands (e.g. execSync('id')). The result is authenticated remote code execution on the Flowise host.
